title: Copilot Chat auto-switches to 1M context after VS Code restart, ignoring selected model setting
body:
* Copilot Chat Extension Version: latest
* VS Code Version: latest
* OS Version: latest
* Feature: Copilot Chat / existing chat session after VS Code restart
* Selected model: GPT 5.5 High 272k

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set Copilot Chat to use GPT 5.5 High with 272k context.
2. Start or continue a Copilot Chat session.
3. Restart VS Code.
4. Continue the existing Copilot Chat session.
5. Check the model/context shown in the Copilot UI.

## Expected Behavior

Copilot should respect the currently selected model/context setting after VS Code restart.

If I selected GPT 5.5 High 272k, continuing an existing session should keep using GPT 5.5 High 272k, unless I explicitly choose another model/context.

## Actual Behavior

After restarting VS Code and continuing an existing session, Copilot appears to ignore the selected setting and silently switches to the 1M context option.

This is especially serious because 1M is much more expensive. The switch is not explicitly confirmed by the user, but the user is still charged for the more expensive option.

## Impact

This caused unexpected billing charges for me. In one case, I paid around $10 because Copilot silently switched to the 1M option.

This makes Copilot unsafe to use with paid models/context tiers, because the UI setting is not reliably respected across VS Code restarts.

## Request

Please investigate why continuing an existing session after VS Code restart can override the user-selected model/context setting.

At minimum, Copilot should never silently switch to a more expensive model/context tier without explicit user confirmation.

title: Flexible "Centered Layout"
body: There is option in View > Appearance called "Centered Layout", it works perfectly while no sidebars are open and vscode is in fullscreen mode, but the moment you open anything alongside it, Centered Layout just becomes annoying to deal with... When you open sidebar, the "Centered Layout" moves content to the center of free space that is left after sidebar opens instead of adjusting itself and hold the center position of entire window. Same goes when you have more than one window opened and try to split-screen. The solution: "Centered Layout" option should add minimum width for content, so after resizing, it doesnt lose 50% of view area for un-needed centering. Also it should be centered to the middle of window and not middle of 'free space left'.

title: Erroneous optional status for y parameter in RepeatedStratifiedKFold.split
body:
### Describe the bug

For context, there is a small difference in the `split` function between the variants of the `KFold` class:

In class `sklearn.model_selection.KFold`, the [split function](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.model_selection.KFold.html#sklearn.model_selection.KFold.split) has an optional parameter `y` (same for class [`sklearn.model_selection.RepeatedKFold`](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.model_selection.RepeatedKFold.html#sklearn.model_selection.RepeatedKFold.split)).

In class `sklearn.model_selection.StratifiedKFold`, the same [parameter `y` is mandatory](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.model_selection.StratifiedKFold.html#sklearn.model_selection.StratifiedKFold.split) because ["Stratification is done based on the y labels"](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/2621573e6/sklearn/model_selection/_split.py#L813). As expected, omitting `y` when calling `split` causes an explicit error:

```
TypeError: StratifiedKFold.split() missing 1 required positional argument: 'y'
```

However [`sklearn.model_selection.RepeatedStratifiedKFold`](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.model_selection.RepeatedStratifiedKFold.html) is also a stratified variant which requires parameter `y`, but the parameter is [erroneously left as optional](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.model_selection.RepeatedStratifiedKFold.html#sklearn.model_selection.RepeatedStratifiedKFold.split). This seems due to the fact this is implemented through a general class [`_UnsupportedGroupCVMixin`](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/2621573e6/sklearn/model_selection/_split.py#L67). As a result, not providing `y` causes an unclear error message inconsistent with the one for `StratifiedKFold` in the same context.





### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```py
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold, RepeatedKFold, StratifiedKFold, RepeatedStratifiedKFold

x = [ 'a '] * 100
y = [ 0 ] * 90 + [ 1 ] * 10

# y is NOT optional -> error 'missing 1 required positional argument: 'y'' as expected 
for i, (train, test) in enumerate(StratifiedKFold(n_splits=2).split(x)):
    print('i =', i, '. train =', train)
    print('i =', i,  '. test =', test)

# y is supposed to be optional according to documentation, but not providing it causes an unclear error message  
for i, (train, test) in enumerate(RepeatedStratifiedKFold(n_splits=2, n_repeats=3).split(x)):
    print('i =', i, '. train =', train)
    print('i =', i,  '. test =', test)
```


### Expected Results

Same error message 'missing 1 required positional argument' in both cases.

### Actual Results

expected error in first example, erroneous behavior in second example.

### Versions

title: Yeo-Johnson inverse_transform fails silently on extreme skew data
body:
### Describe the bug

The Yeo-Johnson is not a surjective transformation for negative lambdas. Therefore, the inverse transformation returns `np.nan` when inverse transforming values outside the range of the transform. This failure is silent, so it took me quite a while of debugging to understand this behavior.

The problematic lines are

https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/8721245511de2f225ff5f9aa5f5fadce663cd4a3/sklearn/preprocessing/_data.py#L3390

and 

https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/8721245511de2f225ff5f9aa5f5fadce663cd4a3/sklearn/preprocessing/_data.py#L3386

in which we might compute `np.power(something_negative, not_integral_value)`, which of course returns `np.nan` as per https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.power.html

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

To reproduce for positive values (there is a similar problem for negative values):

```python
import numpy as np
import sklearn.preprocessing
trans = sklearn.preprocessing.PowerTransformer(method='yeo-johnson')
x = np.array([1,1,1e10]).reshape(-1, 1) # extreme skew
trans.fit(x)
lmbda = trans.lambdas_[0] 
print(lmbda)
assert lmbda < 0 # == -0.096 negative value

# any value `psi` for which lambda*psi+1 <= 0 will result in nan due to lacking support, since the forwards transformation 
# is not surjective on negative lambdas. In this specific case, 10*-0.096 < 1
psi = np.array([10]).reshape(-1, 1)
x = trans.inverse_transform(psi).item()
print(x)
assert np.isnan(x)
```

### Expected Results

 The code should either:

1) validate its inputs and raise an exception
2) validate its inputs and raise a warning
3) fail silently, but have it documented behavior

### Actual Results

It just prints

```
-0.0962322261004418
nan
```

title: Agent terminal gets stuck on interactive package manager prompts (needrestart, debconf, pipe buffering)
body:
## Summary

The agent terminal tool fails to handle two classes of interactive prompts, causing consistent eval failures on terminalbench2 tasks that copilot-cli passes.

## Problem 1: needrestart/debconf TUI prompts

When `apt-get install` triggers needrestart, a "Which services should be restarted?" TUI prompt appears. None of the `detectsHighConfidenceInputPattern()` or `detectsLikelyInputRequiredPattern()` patterns in `outputMonitor.ts` match this format. The `idleSilence` timer fires after 60s, the tool returns "moved to background", and the agent gives up.

**Example**: `nginx-request-logging` — agent ran `apt-get install -y nginx`, needrestart prompted, agent waited 5 minutes then sent an empty `send_to_terminal` and stopped. copilot-cli finished the same task in 33 seconds.

## Problem 2: Pipe buffering hides prompts

When the model pipes an interactive command through `tail`/`head`/`grep`, the pipe buffers all output until the upstream closes. Interactive prompts (like opam `[Y/n]`) never reach the terminal buffer, so the `OutputMonitor` cannot detect them.

**Example**: `compile-compcert` — agent ran `opam install coq -y 2>&1 | tail -20`. The opam depext `[Y/n]` prompt was buffered by `tail` and never visible to the output monitor. The `[Y/n]` pattern *would* match `detectsHighConfidenceInputPattern` if it reached the terminal — the bug is the pipe hiding it.

## Fix

1. Set `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` in the copilot agent terminal env vars (in `toolTerminalCreator.ts`), alongside existing `GIT_PAGER`, `GIT_EDITOR`, etc. This prevents apt/dpkg from ever opening interactive prompts.

2. Add system prompt guidance telling the model to never pipe interactive commands through filters.

## Impact

Found via terminalbench2 eval analysis comparing vscode-copilot-cli vs copilot-cli. These two issues account for 2 of 12 consistent regressions (~13% score gap).

title: Copilot Chat: sequence of dots (`.........`) jumps or bunches in outputs (rendering issue)
body:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version:

- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): multiple models (observed across several models)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open the Copilot Chat extension in VS Code (with default settings).
2. Ask the model to output or paste a line containing repeated periods (for example: `.........`) or view model outputs that include such lines.

Expected behavior:

Actual behavior:

title: BUG: pd.json_normalize has inconsistent validation for non-string keys - accepts integers in some parameter combinations but rejects in others
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd
data = [{"a": 1, 12: "meta_value", "nested": [{"b": 2}]}]
# This works - integer as meta  
pd.json_normalize(data, meta=[12])
# But this fails - integer as meta (with record_path)
pd.json_normalize(data, record_path=["nested"], meta=[12])
```

### Issue Description

pd.json_normalize exhibits inconsistent behavior when using integer keys in the meta parameter:
meta=[12] (without record_path) - WORKS
 record_path=["nested"], meta=[12] - FAILS with TypeError: sequence item 0: expected str instance, int found

### Expected Behavior

This should either be consistently supported or consistently rejected.

### Installed Versions

<details>

Replace this line with the output of pd.show_versions()

</details>

title: `IntervalDtype.__from_arrow__` raises a TypeError when subtype is a nullable ExtensionDtype
body:
```python
In [1]: import pandas as pd

In [3]: import pyarrow as pa

In [5]: from pandas.core.arrays.arrow.extension_types import ArrowIntervalType

In [6]: pd.IntervalDtype(pd.Int64Dtype(), "right").__from_arrow__(pa.array([{"left": 1, "right": 2}], type=ArrowIntervalType(pa.int64(), "right")))
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TypeError                                 Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[6], line 1
----> 1 pd.IntervalDtype(pd.Int64Dtype(), "right").__from_arrow__(pa.array([{"left": 1, "right": 2}], type=ArrowIntervalType(pa.int64(), "right")))

File ~/pandas/core/dtypes/dtypes.py:1476, in IntervalDtype.__from_arrow__(self, array)
   1474 if isinstance(arr, pyarrow.ExtensionArray):
   1475     arr = arr.storage
-> 1476 left = np.asarray(arr.field("left"), dtype=self.subtype)
   1477 right = np.asarray(arr.field("right"), dtype=self.subtype)
   1478 iarr = IntervalArray.from_arrays(left, right, closed=self.closed)

TypeError: Cannot interpret 'Int64Dtype()' as a data type
```

The `left` and `right` instead should be `ExtensionArray`s if `self.subdtype` is an `ExtensionDtype`

title: BUG: Build from source fails for scikit-learn v1.6.1 on Windows 11 with Visual Studio Build Tools 2022, Ninja subprocess error
body:
### Describe the bug

First of all, thank you guys for the fantastic job with Sklearn. 
I'm trying to build from source to start contributing to the project, but it ended with me bringing more issues to you. 
After struggling for some days with this problem, I'm seeking help. Maybe if you have some clue or workaround, I could open a Pull Request with the solution for this.

I am following the guidelines for [Contributing with Scikit-learn](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/developers/contributing.html#contributing), and for that, it is necessary to [Build from source on Windows](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/developers/advanced_installation.html#windows), which recomends install  Build Tools for Visual Studio 2019, but nowadays is not possible to download the 2019 version just the [Build Tools for Visual Studio 2022 installer](https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vs_buildtools.exe).

The installation of Build Tools for Visual Studio 2022 runs smoothly(and also the initialization of its Environment), as well as the creation of the Python virtual environment and the installation of the packages `wheel, numpy, scipy, cython, meson-python, ninja`.

But in the step of building from source using the `pip install --editable`, the build breaks when Compiling C objects after some [C4090 warnings](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-1-c4090?view=msvc-170), throwing an `metadata-generation-failed error` from a subprocess of `ninja build`.

This seems related/similar to issue #31123. Despite not being the same problem, if we find a solution, it may work for both issues.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

I have tried the steps using `pip install` and also `conda-forge` in different versions of Python: pip :{3.10.11, 3.12.7} conda:{ 3.13.2}  to check if it was a problem with Python/pip itself.

1. **Environment Setup:**
- OS:  Windows 11 Pro, Version 24H2, OS build 26100.3476
- System type: 64-bit operating system, x64-based processor
- Installed Visual Studio Build **2022**, version 17.13.5 (It is not possible to download from Microsoft the 2019 version as recommended)
  -  I also restarted the computer after installing (because it is Windows, so you never know)

![VSbuildTools](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3fd83a4d-0451-4c79-97f4-ff6d796a75d9)

2. **Project directory**
- `C:\Users\vitor.pohlenz\Documents\Python\vpz\scikit-learn`

3. **Installing compiler**
Installed [Microsoft build Tools 2022](https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vs_buildtools.exe)

Initialized the enviroment:
```
SET DISTUTILS_USE_SDK=1

"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools\VC\Auxiliary\Build\vcvarsall.bat" x64
```
Which gives the output:

```
**********************************************************************
** Visual Studio 2022 Developer Command Prompt v17.13.5
** Copyright (c) 2022 Microsoft Corporation
**********************************************************************
[vcvarsall.bat] Environment initialized for: 'x64'
```
 
4. **Steps Using pip**, the same error occurs using Python 3.10 or 3.12. I will provide the steps and logs just for 3.10 for the sake of simplicity.

Navigated to the project directory: `C:\Users\vitor.pohlenz\Documents\Python\vpz\scikit-learn`

Created the Python virtual environment, upgraded pip, and installed the dependencies
```
py -3.10 -m venv envsklearn

envsklearn\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install --upgrade pip

envsklearn\Scripts\activate

pip install wheel numpy scipy cython meson-python ninja
```

Until here, everything works as expected. But when building from source, in the command below, the error occurs:

```
pip install --editable . --verbose --no-build-isolation --config-settings editable-verbose=true
```

5. **Using conda forge**:
To check if it wasn't a problem with pip I have also used `conda-forge`, as described in the [Building from Source tutorial](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/developers/advanced_installation.html#building-from-source):

`conda create -n sklearn-env -c conda-forge python numpy scipy cython meson-python ninja`
`conda activate sklearn-env`
`pip install --editable . --verbose --no-build-isolation --config-settings editable-verbose=true`

> Note: I used 2 different environment names to avoid confusion: `envsklearn` is the `venv` that uses `pip`, and `sklearn-env` is the `conda env`

### Expected Results

The build of scikit-learn should finish and install without error, or at least with a few warnings.

### Actual Results

## Using pip install
It seems that the Meson build system finds the compiler for C, C+++, and also the `ninja.exe` in the environment. But ninja subprocess breaks when compiling `C object sklearn/metrics/_dist_metrics.cp310-win_amd64.pyd.p/meson-generated_sklearn_metrics__dist_metrics.pyx.c.obj`

title: Metadata not routed to transformers in pipeline during cross validation.
body:
### Describe the bug

When using a pipeline with transformers in combination with cross validation, it seems that metadata is not correctly routed to the transformers during prediction. I would expect, that if `set_transform_request` is set, that this is honored when calling predict on the pipeline.

**Edit:** At least according to the code this is a known limitation. Although I couldn't find an issue tracking the progress on this.
https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/9028b518e7a906a806a1dc8994f2714cc980c941/sklearn/model_selection/_validation.py#L362C1-L367C14

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import sklearn
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.datasets import make_regression
from sklearn.base import TransformerMixin, _MetadataRequester
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_validate

print(sklearn.__version__)

sklearn.set_config(enable_metadata_routing=True)


class DummyTransfomerWithMetadata(TransformerMixin, _MetadataRequester):

    def fit(self, X, y=None, metadata=None):
        return self

    def transform(self, X, y=None, metadata=None):
        print(f"Received {metadata=}")
        return X

    # We need to explicitly implement fit_transform,
    # otherwise transform will not receive metadata during fit
    def fit_transform(self, X, y=None, metadata=None):
        return self.transform(X, y, metadata)


X, y = make_regression()

transformer = DummyTransfomerWithMetadata()
transformer.set_fit_request(metadata=True)
transformer.set_transform_request(metadata=True)


pipe = Pipeline([
    ("transformer", transformer),
    ("clf", LinearRegression())
])


print(f"--- Cross validation ---")
cross_validate(
    pipe, X, y, params={"metadata": "Some metadata"}, cv=2
)
```

### Expected Results

```
1.7.0
--- Cross validation ---
Received metadata='Some metadata'   # Fit 
Received metadata='Some metadata'   # Predict
Received metadata='Some metadata'
Received metadata='Some metadata'
```

### Actual Results

```
1.7.0
--- Cross validation ---
Received metadata='Some metadata'
Received metadata=None
Received metadata='Some metadata'
Received metadata=None
```

title: Unpickling ColumnTransformer fitted in 1.6.1 fails in 1.7.1 with AttributeError: _RemainderColsList
body:
### Describe the bug

**Summary** 

A `ColumnTransformer` pickled with **scikit-learn 1.6.1** cannot be unpickled with **1.7.1** (and other versions > 1.6.1). The unpickling fails before any method call with:

```bash
AttributeError: Can't get attribute '_RemainderColsList' on <module 'sklearn.compose._column_transformer' from '.../site-packages/sklearn/compose/_column_transformer.py'>
```

This makes it impossible to load persisted pipelines across these versions when ColumnTransformer was used.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

## Run it with scikit-learn==1.6.1
```
import pickle
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, StandardScaler

# Minimal toy data
df = pd.DataFrame({"num": [1.0, 2.0, 3.5], "cat": ["a", "b", "a"]})

# Minimal ColumnTransformer
ct = ColumnTransformer(
    transformers=[
        ("num", StandardScaler(), ["num"]),
        ("cat", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore", sparse_output=False), ["cat"]),
    ], remainder="passthrough"
)

# Fit and pickle
ct.fit(df)
with open("column_transformer.pkl", "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(ct, f)

```
## Run with scikit-learn > 1.6.1
```
import pickle
import pandas as pd

# Unpickle the fitted transformer
with open("column_transformer.pkl", "rb") as f:
    ct = pickle.load(f)

# Use on small test data (includes an unseen category "c")
df2 = pd.DataFrame({"num": [0.0, 1.0], "cat": ["a", "c"]})
X = ct.transform(df2)

```

### Expected Results

A ColumnTransformer fitted and persisted in 1.6.1 can be loaded in 1.7.1 and used normally (e.g., transform), or—if cross-version unpickling is intentionally unsupported—clear guidance in release notes and/or a compatibility shim to avoid a hard failure on import.

### Actual Results

Unpickling fails immediately with AttributeError (below), seemingly because a private helper `_RemainderColsList` referenced in the pickle no longer exists / was moved in `sklearn.compose._column_transformer` in 1.7.x.

`AttributeError: Can't get attribute '_RemainderColsList' on <module 'sklearn.compose._column_transformer' from '/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/miniforge/base/envs/ml-py310/lib/python3.10/site-packages/sklearn/compose/_column_transformer.py'>`

title: BUG: DataFrame.loc[] returns object type instead of float
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
from pandas import DataFrame


df_e = DataFrame([["x", 10.0, 20.0], ["y", 30.0, 40.0]])
print(df_e.loc[0, slice(1, 3)])
```

### Issue Description

When selecting columns using slices from a DataFrame that contains mixed data types, the resulting columns always have the dtype object, even when all the selected elements share the same data type (for example, float64). # Current Output: 
1    10.0
2    20.0
Name: 0, dtype: object


# Expected Output:

Wrong chance_level plot in PrecisionRecallDisplay when using Pytorch data
body: ### Describe the bug and give evidence about its user-facing impact The chance_level line drawn by `PrecisionRecallDisplay.from_predictions` when `plot_chance_level=True` is not correct when `y_true` is an instance of `pytorch.Tensor`. ### Steps/Code to Reproduce ```python # pytorch==2.8.0 from sklearn.metrics import PrecisionRecallDisplay import torch torch.manual_seed(42) y_true = torch.cat([torch.ones(40), torch.zeros(60)]) # Chance level equal 0.4 y_score = torch.rand_like(y_true) PrecisionRecallDisplay.from_predictions(y_true, y_score, plot_chance_level=True) # Wrong PrecisionRecallDisplay.from_predictions(y_true.numpy(), y_score, plot_chance_level=True) # Correct ``` ### Expected Results Correct chance classifier line at y 0.4 with y_true as a numpy array. ### Actual Results Wrong chance classifier line at y 0.0 with y_true as a pytorch tensor. ### Versions ```shell System: python: 3.13.5 | packaged by Anaconda, Inc. | (main, Jun 12 2025, 16:37:03) [MSC v.1929 64 bit (AMD64)] executable: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\python.exe machine: Windows-11-10.0.26200-SP0 Python dependencies: sklearn: 1.7.1 pip: 25.1 setuptools: 78.1.1 numpy: 2.1.2 scipy: 1.16.1 Cython: None pandas: 2.3.3 matplotlib: 3.10.5 joblib: 1.5.1 threadpoolctl: 3.6.0 Built with OpenMP: True threadpoolctl info: user_api: openmp internal_api: openmp num_threads: 6 prefix: libiomp filepath: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\Lib\site-packages\torch\lib\libiomp5md.dll version: None user_api: openmp internal_api: openmp num_threads: 1 prefix: libiomp filepath: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\Lib\site-packages\torch\lib\libiompstubs5md.dll version: None user_api: blas internal_api: openblas num_threads: 12 prefix: libscipy_openblas filepath: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\Lib\site-packages\numpy.libs\libscipy_openblas64_-c16e4918366c6bc1f1cd71e28ca36fc0.dll version: 0.3.27 threading_layer: pthreads architecture: Haswell user_api: openmp internal_api: openmp num_threads: 12 prefix: vcomp filepath: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\Lib\site-packages\sklearn\.libs\vcomp140.dll version: None user_api: blas internal_api: openblas num_threads: 12 prefix: libscipy_openblas filepath: C:\Users\lucas\miniconda\envs\pytorch28\Lib\site-packages\scipy.libs\libscipy_openblas-6b2103f2ae4d8547998b5d188e9801fb.dll version: 0.3.28 threading_layer: pthreads architecture: Haswell ``` ### Interest in fixing the bug Working on this, the root cause is due to these reasons: - pytorch (and potentially other array libraries) defines scalars in such a way that their hash is different from their value and same values may have different hashes; - `PrecisionRecallDisplay.from_predictions` relies on `Counter` class to compare `y_true` entries with `pos_label` values in order to generate `prevalence_pos_label` (chance level). https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/646da0f072a8afef6a980aa427a710311e67eb9d/sklearn/metrics/_plot/precision_recall_curve.py#L563-L565 - `Counter` is not able to group objects with same values since they have different hash values. Comparing the values from `y_true` directly to `pos_label` to calculate `prevalence_pos_label` should be enough to fix this.

title: Potential NameError in _gpc.py when eval_gradient is False
body:
### Describe the bug and give evidence about its user-facing impact

Hi! While exploring the gaussian_process module for GSoC preparation, I noticed a potential NameError in _gpc.py around line 399. If eval_gradient is False, K_gradient is not defined, but the code later attempts to access it in a loop. I have a fix ready and would love to submit a PR if this is confirmed as an issue!

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

import numpy as np
from sklearn.gaussian_process import GaussianProcessClassifier
from sklearn.gaussian_process.kernels import RBF

# 1. Setup simple data
X = np.array([[0], [1], [2]])
y = np.array([0, 1, 0])

# 2. Fit the model
gpc = GaussianProcessClassifier(kernel=RBF())
gpc.fit(X, y)

# 3. Trigger the log_marginal_likelihood with eval_gradient=False
# This should theoretically fail at line 399 in _gpc.py 
# because K_gradient is accessed but not defined.
theta = gpc.kernel_.theta
try:
    lml = gpc.log_marginal_likelihood(theta, eval_gradient=False)
    print(f"LML: {lml}")
except NameError as e:
    print(f"Caught expected NameError: {e}")

### Expected Results

The function should return the log-marginal-likelihood (Z) without attempting to calculate or access gradients when eval_gradient is set to False.

### Actual Results

>A NameError: name 'K_gradient' is not defined occurs when the loop at line 398 attempts to access K_gradient, because the variable was only initialized inside the if eval_gradient: block.

title: Markdown `editor.colorDecorators: false` not honored on startup; likely race condition
body:
As per #233211, the following VS Code setting turns off inappropriately showing strings like `#xxxxxx` as a CSS color indicator in Markdown (particularly ironic and irritating when the Markdown is a list of VS Code bug titles):

```json
  "[markdown]": {
    "editor.colorDecorators": false
  },
```

To reproduce (nondeterministic, suggesting a race condition):

1. Set `"editor.colorDecorators": false` in `settings.json` as above.
2. Create a Markdown page full of `#xxxxxx` references, such as these:
    ```markdown
    * [GitHub Copilot Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString') when resetting. #319626](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319626)
    * [GitHub Copilot Markdown paste discards HTML tag from inline code #319733](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319733)
    * [[major] v1.123 breaks model-selection ability when resubmitting prompt #319743](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319743)
    ```
3. Restart VS Code.

**Expected:** Strings such as ` #319626` do _not_ show a color decorator because `editor.colorDecorators` is set to `false`.

**Actual:** Strings such as ` #319626` often _do_ show a color decorator.

**Workaround:** When this occurs, switch to another tab and then switch back. Upon switching back to the original tab, the color decorators disappear.

**Discussion:** This does not always occur. In my case I'm usually loading large workspaces with many files open when it happens. This, along with the fact that it corrects itself after switching tabs, leads me to believe it's some sort of race condition checking the settings upon first load. Nevertheless, it is irritating to have to manually switch tabs just to get the colors to go away. (They shouldn't have been there in the first place, as discussed in #233211; this [should have been made an opt-in](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/233211#issuecomment-2460269705) for languages other than CSS.)

### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "id": ["A", "B"],
    "arr": [np.array([1.0, 2.0]), np.array([3.0, 4.0])],  # numpy arrays
})

1. A column containing numpy arrays (object dtype)
2. A SparseArray column

title: Chat: system notification XML tags rendered raw in conversation
body: ## Problem When the Copilot agent runtime sends system notifications (e.g., background agent completion), the `` XML tags are rendered as raw text in the chat conversation instead of being hidden or styled appropriately. ## Expected behavior System notifications wrapped in `...` tags should either be: - Hidden from the visible conversation (they are metadata for the model, not the user) - Or rendered as a styled, dismissible notification element ## Actual behavior The raw XML tags appear verbatim in the chat output, e.g.: ``` Background agent explore-session-artifacts completed. ``` ## Context These notifications are injected by the agent runtime as non-billable system messages to inform the model about async events (shell command completions, background agent results). They are not intended to be user-facing content.

title: BUG: read_csv() into FloatingArrays converts np.nan into pd.NA, even with distinguish_nan_and_na=True
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [ ] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import io
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

csv = """,x
a,1.0
b,
c,nan"""

with pd.option_context("future.distinguish_nan_and_na", True):
    s = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv), index_col=0, dtype={0: str, 1: pd.Float64Dtype()},
                     keep_default_na=False, na_values=[""])["x"]
s
```

### Issue Description

Working with data having both `pd.NA` and `np.nan` is possible and, at least until pandas2, it was possible to save such dataframes as CSV in a format that keeps a distinction between the two (see #65227).

However, in the example above pandas fails with:

```
ValueError: Unable to parse string "nan" at position 2
```

The current workaround consists in reading the CSV as string, and manually map its value to floats:
```python
import io
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

csv = """,x
a,1.0
b,
c,nan"""


def convert_nan(val: str):
    match val:
        case "":
            return pd.NA
        case _:
            return np.float64(val) # includes np.nan

with pd.option_context("future.distinguish_nan_and_na", True):
    s1 = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv), index_col=0, dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)["x"]
    s2 = s1.map(convert_nan)
    s3 = s2.convert_dtypes()
s1,s2,s3
```
returns:
```
(a    1.0
 b       
 c    nan
 Name: x, dtype: str,
 a     1.0
 b    <NA>
 c     NaN
 Name: x, dtype: object,
 a     1.0
 b    <NA>

I would expect `read_csv()` to distinguish between `NA` and `NaN` in the columns with `dtype=Float64`. Even more so if `distinguish_nan_and_na=True`.

title: Adding the first root folder to an empty-window workspace restarts the extension host, killing in-progress Copilot Chat sessions
body:
### Description

Type: Bug / UX papercut

VS Code version: 
Version: 1.123.0 (user setup)
Commit: 6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8
Date: 2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00
Electron: 42.2.0
ElectronBuildId: 14159160
Chromium: 148.0.7778.97
Node.js: 24.15.0
V8: 14.8.178.14-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
OS: Windows

Describe the bug
When a window has no folder open (empty workspace) and you add a folder via Add Folder to Workspace (workbench.action.addRootFolder), VS Code transitions the workspace and restarts the extension host. This triggers a "Please confirm restart of extensions — A session is in progress" dialog and, if confirmed, terminates the active GitHub Copilot Chat session.

Steps to reproduce

Launch VS Code with no folder open (empty window).
Start a Copilot Chat session and do some work.
Add Folder to Workspace → pick any folder.
Observe the "Please confirm restart of extensions" dialog; confirming restarts the extension host and loses the chat session.
Expected behavior
Adding a folder to an existing window should not force an extension-host restart that destroys in-progress extension state (like a Copilot Chat conversation). Going from 0→1 folders should behave like N→N+1 folders, which does not restart the host.

Actual behavior
The 0→1 folder transition (empty window → single-root/multi-root workspace) restarts the extension host, killing the chat session. There's no path to mount the first folder without losing session state.

Impact
Users who start a chat in an empty window — e.g., during design/planning before a repo exists — must choose between keeping their conversation or working with the folder mounted in the Explorer. Long planning sessions are lost on folder add.

Possible improvements

Preserve extension-host state across the empty→single-folder transition (avoid the restart), or
Allow extensions to persist/restore session state across the transition, or
Make the restart non-destructive for chat (serialize + rehydrate the conversation).

title: The covariance matrix is incorrect in BayesianRidge
body:
### Describe the bug

The posterior covariance matrix in `BayesianRidge`, attribute `sigma_`,  is incorrect when `n_features > n_samples`. This is because the posterior covariance requires the full svd, while the current code uses the reduced svd.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import numpy as np
from sklearn.linear_model import BayesianRidge
from sklearn import datasets

# on main
X, y = datasets.make_regression(n_samples=10, n_features=20)
n_features = X.shape[1]
reg = BayesianRidge(fit_intercept=False).fit(X, y)
covariance_matrix = np.linalg.inv(
    reg.lambda_ * np.identity(n_features) + reg.alpha_ * np.dot(X.T, X)
)
np.allclose(reg.sigma_, covariance_matrix)
```

### Expected Results

True

### Actual Results

False

### Versions

```shell
1.7.dev0
```

title: Check `pos_label` present in `y_true` in metric functions
body:
Noticed while working on https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/pull/30508#discussion_r2158871194

Currently the following metric functions do not explicitly check that `pos_label` is present in `y_true`:

* `roc_curve`
* `precision_recall_curve`
* `det_curve`
* `brier_score_loss`

AFAICT all (?) other classification metrics (e.g., `recall_score`, `precision_score`), including ranking metric `average_precision_score` explicitly check that `pos_label` is present in `y_true`:

e.g. this is the error from `recall_score`/`precision_score`/`f1` family:
```
        if y_type == "binary":
            if len(present_labels) == 2 and pos_label not in present_labels:
>               raise ValueError(
                    f"pos_label={pos_label} is not a valid label. It should be "
                    f"one of {present_labels}"
                )
E               ValueError: pos_label=2 is not a valid label. It should be one of [0, 1]
```

`roc_curve` and `precision_recall_curve` do not explicitly check this, they do *warn* (no error) that there are no 'positive' samples in `y_true`:

```
        if tps[-1] <= 0:
>           warnings.warn(
                "No positive samples in y_true, true positive value should be meaningless",
                UndefinedMetricWarning,
            )
E           sklearn.exceptions.UndefinedMetricWarning: No positive samples in y_true, true positive value should be meaningless
```

Similarly, for `det_curve` this results in an invalid divide warning (we divide by 0):
```
File ~/Documents/dev/scikit-learn/sklearn/metrics/_ranking.py:418, in det_curve(y_true, y_score, pos_label, sample_weight, drop_intermediate)
    415 sl = slice(first_ind, last_ind)
    417 # reverse the output such that list of false positives is decreasing
--> 418 return (fps[sl][::-1] / n_count, fns[sl][::-1] / p_count, thresholds[sl][::-1])

RuntimeWarning: invalid value encountered in divide
```

`brier_score_loss` gives no error and no warning. `_validate_binary_probabilistic_prediction` does the following

https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/bde701db13cebd5d5ed0bc049fb0bc9693c6c5dc/sklearn/metrics/_classification.py#L3468-L3469

which just results in `y_true` being all 0's.

For completeness I'm just going to reference #18101, even though I don't think it directly affects this issue; regardless of `pos_label` meaning, the question of whether to check it is present in `y_true` is the same. Just a reference for those who were involved in that discussion, e.g., @glemaitre 😬

title: Webview resource cache grows unboundedly: media-preview cache-busts with Date.now(), service worker never evicts (21+ GB observed)
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes
(media-preview and the webview service worker are built-in, so `--disable-extensions` does not affect the leak.)

- VS Code Version: 1.123.2 (commit 3c631b164c239e7aeaaae7c626b46c527b361af2, x64)
- OS Version: Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200 (also observed under WSL Remote – Ubuntu)

## Summary

`%APPDATA%\Code\WebStorage\<n>\CacheStorage` grew to **23.5 GB** on my machine.
I parsed the Chromium simple-cache entry keys to find out why. The root cause is a
combination of two pieces of built-in code:

1. **media-preview cache-busts with the current time, not the file version.**
   `extensions/media-preview/src/imagePreview/index.ts` (`getResourcePath`) appends
   `?version=${Date.now()}` on **every render**. Every time an image is opened or
   re-rendered, the URL is brand new — even if the file is unchanged.

2. **The webview service worker caches every response and never evicts.**
   `src/vs/workbench/contrib/webview/browser/pre/service-worker.js` does
   `cache.put(event.request, ...)` for every 200 response with an ETag, keyed by the
   full URL including the query string. The file contains no eviction, expiry, or
   quota logic. The `activate` handler doesn't even delete caches from previous
   `VERSION`s — my disk holds both `vscode-resource-cache-4` (2.09 GB) and
   `vscode-resource-cache-5` (21.38 GB).

Combined effect: **every view of an image permanently writes a full copy to disk**,
and each copy is dead on arrival — the `Date.now()` query string guarantees no future
request can ever hit it.

## Evidence (from parsing the cache entry keys)

My 21.38 GB cache origin contains 6,413 entries:

- They map to only **1,947 unique files** (1,013 files cached ≥ 2 times).
- The most-viewed image was cached **86 times within a single hour** (one evening,
  up to 9 copies in one minute) — per-render, not per-modification.
- In a sample of the 200 most-duplicated paths, **81% point to files that no longer
  exist** on disk.
- Zero video/audio entries: `<video>` uses Range requests (206), which the SW marks
  `no-store` and the Cache API cannot store anyway. The leak therefore only bites
  image-heavy workflows and grows slowly enough to evade detection — which is
  presumably why earlier reports (#131226, #166632 [145 GB], #152519 [500 GB]) died
  as "cannot reproduce" / stale.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Note the size of `%APPDATA%\Code\WebStorage` (`du -sh ~/.config/Code/WebStorage` on Linux).
2. Launch `code --disable-extensions` and open a large PNG (e.g. 20 MB) in the
   built-in image preview.
3. Close the tab and reopen the same file 10 times.
4. The folder grows by ~10 × the image size. None of the new entries can ever be
   read back (each was stored under a unique `?version=<timestamp>` URL), and they
   are never cleaned up.

## Expected

Either of:
- media-preview keys the cache-busting parameter on the file's mtime instead of
  `Date.now()`, so unchanged files reuse one cache entry; and/or
- the service worker evicts stale versions of the same base URL when caching a new
  one, applies an LRU/size cap to `vscode-resource-cache-*`, and deletes
  previous-`VERSION` caches on activate.

## Related

#131226 (closed as stale, "needs repro"), #166632 (145 GB, closed as duplicate),
#152519 (500 GB, closed as duplicate), #310384 (open, 30 GB via Remote-SSH — same
mechanism; this report adds the root-cause analysis).

title: BUG: pd.Grouper converts PyArrow timestamp to numpy datetime64
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd
import io

data = '''agency_cd,site_no,datetime,tz_cd,144166_00060,144166_00060_cd,144167_00065,144167_00065_cd
USGS,9333500,2001-05-07 01:00:00,MDT,71.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-07 01:15:00,MDT,71.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-07 01:30:00,MDT,71.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-07 01:45:00,MDT,70.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-07 02:00:00,MDT,70.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-08 02:15:00,MDT,69.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-08 02:30:00,MDT,70.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-08 02:45:00,MDT,70.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-08 03:00:00,MDT,70.0,A:[91],,'''

df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(data), dtype_backend='pyarrow', engine='pyarrow')

def to_denver_time(df_, time_col, tz_col):
    return (df_
     .assign(**{tz_col: df_[tz_col].replace('MDT', 'MST7MDT')})
     .groupby(tz_col)
     [time_col]
     .transform(lambda s: pd.to_datetime(s)
         .dt.tz_localize(s.name, ambiguous=True)
         .dt.tz_convert('America/Denver'))
     .astype('timestamp[ns, America/Denver][pyarrow]')
    )

def tweak_river(df_):
    return (df_
     .assign(datetime=to_denver_time(df_, 'datetime', 'tz_cd'))
     .rename(columns={'144166_00060': 'cfs'})
     .loc[:, ['datetime', 'cfs']]
     .set_index('datetime')
    )

dd = tweak_river(df)

# Check the dtype before grouping
print("Before pd.Grouper:")
print(f"Index dtype: {dd.index.dtype}")

# Use pd.Grouper to group by day
result = dd.groupby(pd.Grouper(freq='D')).median(numeric_only=True)

# Check the dtype after grouping
print("\nAfter pd.Grouper:")
print(f"Index dtype: {result.index.dtype}")
```

### Issue Description

When using `pd.Grouper` with a PyArrow-backed timestamp column, the resulting index is automatically converted from the PyArrow dtype (`timestamp[ns, America/Denver][pyarrow]`) to the numpy-backed dtype (`datetime64[ns, America/Denver]`). This conversion happens silently and is inconsistent with the goal of maintaining PyArrow types throughout the analysis pipeline.

This affects users working with PyArrow backend who want to maintain PyArrow types for performance and consistency reasons.

### Expected Behavior

The resulting index after using `pd.Grouper` should maintain the original PyArrow dtype (`timestamp[ns, America/Denver][pyarrow]`) instead of converting to numpy's `datetime64[ns, America/Denver]`.

title: Gaussian Mixture: Diagonal covariance vectors might contain unreasonably negative values when the input datatype is np.float32
body:
### Describe the bug

The Gaussian Mixture implementation shows numerical instabilities on single-precision floating point input numbers, that even large values of the regularization parameter reg_covar (like 0.1) cannot mitigate.

More specifically, diagonal covariance elements must not be negative. However, due to the numerical instabilities intrinsic to floating point arithmetic, they might end up being tiny negative numbers that reg_covar must compensate.
It turns out that, for some input float32 , the covariance can reach the unreasonable value of -0.99999979.
This is because squaring float32 numbers significantly magnifies their precision errors.

The proposed solution consists in converting float32 values to float64 before squaring them.
Care must be taken to not increase memory consumption in the overall process.
Hence, as avgX_means is equal to avg_means2, the return value can be simplified.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import numpy as np
from sklearn.mixture import GaussianMixture

title: BUG: `Series.values` returns inconsistent type (`ndarray` vs `MaskedArray`) depending on whether DataFrame rows are pre-sorted by groupby key
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

print(pd.__version__)

bins = np.linspace(0., 100., 101)
xc   = 0.5 * (bins[1:] + bins[:-1])

for label, x, y in [
    ("non-empty, y sorted by bin",   np.ma.array([0.1, 0.2, 0.3]), np.array([25., 50., 75.])),
    ("non-empty, y unsorted by bin", np.ma.array([0.1, 0.2, 0.3]), np.array([50., 75., 25.])),
    ("empty",                        np.ma.array([], dtype=float),  np.array([])),
]:
    df = pd.DataFrame()
    df["x"] = x
    df["y"] = y
    df = df.loc[:, ["x", "y"]]
    df.reset_index(inplace=True)
    df["bin"] = pd.cut(df["y"].values, bins=bins, labels=[str(c) for c in xc])
    for name, grp in df.groupby("bin", observed=False):
        print(f"{label}: {type(grp['x'].values).__name__}")
        break
```
prints
```
3.1.0.dev0+824.gb00d4f6710
non-empty, y sorted by bin: MaskedArray
non-empty, y unsorted by bin: ndarray
empty: MaskedArray
```

### Issue Description

When a `numpy.ma.MaskedArray` column is stored in a DataFrame and groups are iterated via `groupby(..., observed=False)`, the type returned by `Series.values` depends on whether the rows happen to be pre-sorted by the groupby key and on whether the DataFrame is empty. The same column, same dtype, same group, just different row ordering or DataFrame size, produces either `ndarray` or `MaskedArray`.

### Expected Behavior

`Series.values` should return a consistent type regardless of whether the underlying DataFrame rows are sorted, unsorted, or empty. Given the column dtype is numeric (e.g. `float64`), `ndarray` is the expected return type in all three cases.

title: GaussianMixture log-probabilities are numerically inaccurate
body:
### Describe the bug

While building an extension to (already fitted) GaussianMixture models (https://github.com/JohannesBuchner/askcarl/),
I was using https://hypothesis.readthedocs.io/ for testing and came across numerical inaccuracies in the log-probabilities computed by scikit-learn's GaussianMixture. These already occur with single components, where one can take  scipy.stats.multivariate_normal.logpdf as the ground truth.

The inaccuracies appear in the function `_estimate_log_gaussian_prob`.

The log-probabilities can be off by 0.2 (see the very last example), which really is not small if those probabilities are used for likelihoods.

I uploaded the full script at https://github.com/JohannesBuchner/gmm-tests/blob/main/test_sklearn.py but the important excerpts are below. A unrelated issue I have is that I want to build sklearn.mixture.GaussianMixture from scratch without fitting it, but .score and .predict_proba always give 0 and 1, respectively. Is there a initialization step I am missing to be allowed to use these functions?

EDIT (by @ogrisel): here is a copy of the reproducer (to make this report self-contained):

<details>

```python

import numpy as np
from numpy import array
from scipy.stats import multivariate_normal
from scipy.special import logsumexp
from numpy.testing import assert_allclose
from hypothesis import given, strategies as st, example, settings
from hypothesis.extra.numpy import arrays
import sklearn.mixture
from  sklearn.mixture._gaussian_mixture import _estimate_log_gaussian_prob

def valid_QR(vectors):
    q, r = np.linalg.qr(vectors)
    return q.shape == vectors.shape and np.all(np.abs(np.diag(r)) > 1e-3) and np.all(np.abs(np.diag(r)) < 1000)

def make_covariance_matrix_via_QR(normalisations, vectors):
    q, r = np.linalg.qr(vectors)
    orthogonal_vectors = q @ np.diag(np.diag(r))
    cov = orthogonal_vectors @ np.diag(normalisations) @ orthogonal_vectors.T
    return cov

def valid_covariance_matrix(A, min_std=1e-6):
    if not np.isfinite(A).all():
        return False
    #if not np.std(A) > min_std:
    #    return False
    if (np.diag(A) <= min_std).any():
        return False

    try:
        np.linalg.inv(np.linalg.inv(A))
    except np.linalg.LinAlgError:
        return False

    try:
        multivariate_normal(mean=np.zeros(len(A)), cov=A)
    except ValueError:
        return False

    return True

# Strategy to generate arbitrary dimensionality mean and covariance
@st.composite
def mean_and_cov(draw):
    dim = draw(st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=10))  # Arbitrary dimensionality
    mu = draw(arrays(np.float64, (dim,), elements=st.floats(-10, 10)))  # Mean vector
    eigval = draw(arrays(np.float64, (dim,), elements=st.floats(1e-6, 10)))
    vectors = draw(arrays(np.float64, (dim,dim), elements=st.floats(-10, 10)).filter(valid_QR))
    cov = make_covariance_matrix_via_QR(eigval, vectors)
    x = draw(arrays(np.float64, (2, dim), elements=st.floats(-1e6, 1e6)))
    return dim, mu, cov, x

).via('discovered failure')
def test_single(mean_cov):
    # a askcarl with one component must behave the same as a single gaussian
    ndim, mu, cov, x = mean_cov
    ncomponents = 1
    if not valid_covariance_matrix(cov):
        return

    rv_truth = multivariate_normal(mu, cov)
    covs = [cov]
    means = [mu]
    weights = [1.0]
    assert x.shape == (2, ndim), (x.shape, 2, ndim)

    truth_logp = rv_truth.logpdf(x)
    assert truth_logp.shape == (len(x),)
    truth_p = rv_truth.pdf(x)

    precisions = [np.linalg.inv(cov) for cov in covs]

    # initialize GMM:
    skgmm = sklearn.mixture.GaussianMixture(
        n_components=ncomponents, weights_init=weights,
        means_init=means, precisions_init=precisions)
    skgmm._initialize(np.zeros((1, 1)), None)
    skgmm._set_parameters((weights, np.array(means), covs, skgmm.precisions_cholesky_))
    assert_allclose(skgmm.weights_, weights)
    assert_allclose(skgmm.means_, means)
    assert_allclose(skgmm.covariances_, covs)
    print('input:', x, skgmm.weights_, skgmm.means_, 'covs:', skgmm.covariances_, skgmm.precisions_cholesky_)
    print('expectation:', truth_p, 'log:', truth_logp)

    # compute probability:
    sk_logp = _estimate_log_gaussian_prob(x, skgmm.means_, skgmm.precisions_cholesky_, 'full').flatten()
    print('actual (_estimate_log_gaussian_prob):', sk_logp)
    assert sk_logp.shape == (len(x),), (sk_logp.shape, len(x))
    assert_allclose(truth_logp, sk_logp, atol=1e-4, rtol=1e-4)
    sk_logp1, sk_logp2 = skgmm._estimate_log_prob_resp(x)
    print('actual (_estimate_log_prob_resp):', sk_logp1, sk_logp2)
    assert_allclose(truth_logp, sk_logp1, atol=1e-4, rtol=1e-4)
    #assert_allclose(truth_logp, sk_logp2)

    sk_logp = skgmm.score(x)
    sk_p = skgmm.predict_proba(x)
    print('actual:', sk_p, 'log', sk_logp)

    # TODO: enable these two tests as well
    # assert_allclose(truth_logp, sk_logp, atol=1e-2, rtol=1e-2)  # -> sklearn-GMM gives score=0
    # assert_allclose(truth_p, sk_p, atol=1e-300, rtol=1e-4)  # -> sklearn-GMM gives p=1

```

</details>

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

1. test setup, given means `mu`, covariances `covs` and its inversion `precisions` (values are listed further down).
```
    skgmm = sklearn.mixture.GaussianMixture(
        n_components=ncomponents, weights_init=weights,
        means_init=means, precisions_init=precisions)
    skgmm._initialize(np.zeros((1, 1)), None)
    skgmm._set_parameters((weights, np.array(means), covs, skgmm.precisions_cholesky_))
```

2. compute logprob:
```
    truth_logp = rv_truth.logpdf(x)   # expectation
    sk_logp = _estimate_log_gaussian_prob(x, skgmm.means_, skgmm.precisions_cholesky_, 'full').flatten()   # actual
```

3. test:

title: VS Code Doesn't Show "Use Workspace Version" for TypeScript in Multi-Root Workspaces or  in Monorepo Setup
body:
# VS Code Workspace Uses Bundled TypeScript Instead of Project TypeScript in Multi-Root Workspace

## Description

When working in a multi-root VS Code workspace, TypeScript language features appear to use VS Code's bundled TypeScript version instead of the TypeScript version installed in the project that owns the currently open file.

This causes diagnostics to differ between:

* Opening the project folder directly
* Opening the same project inside a multi-root workspace

## Workspace Structure

```text
xxx-system-workspace
├── xxx-laravel-api-phase-two
└── xxx-next-js-phase-two
    ├── package.json
    ├── tsconfig.json
    └── node_modules/typescript
```

The Laravel project does not use TypeScript.

The Next.js project contains:

```json
{
  "devDependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5"
  }
}
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a multi-root workspace containing:

   * A Laravel project
   * A Next.js project with TypeScript installed locally

2. Open the `.code-workspace` file in VS Code.

3. Open a `.tsx` file inside the Next.js project.

4. Run:

```
TypeScript: Go to Project Configuration
```

VS Code correctly opens the Next.js project's `tsconfig.json`.

5. Run:

```
TypeScript: Select TypeScript Version
```

VS Code offers:

```
Use VS Code's Version 6.0.0-dev
```

and appears to use bundled TypeScript diagnostics instead of the project's installed TypeScript version.

## Expected Behavior

Since VS Code correctly identifies the owning `tsconfig.json`, it should automatically use the TypeScript SDK installed in the owning project:

```
xxx-next-js-phase-two/node_modules/typescript
```

or at minimum clearly expose the workspace version associated with that project.

## Actual Behavior

VS Code appears to prefer the bundled TypeScript version for language features when opened through a multi-root workspace.

As a result:

* Diagnostics differ from project builds.
* Diagnostics differ from opening the Next.js folder directly.
* TypeScript behavior changes depending on whether the project is opened standalone or as part of a workspace.

## Additional Notes

Opening only the Next.js folder causes VS Code to correctly use the project's TypeScript version and the issue disappears.

This suggests the problem is related to TypeScript SDK resolution within multi-root workspaces rather than project configuration.

title: FeatureUnion with polars output can error due to duplicate column names
body:
### Describe the bug

FeatureUnion concatenates outputs of its transformers _before_ the `set_output` wrapper renames columns based on `get_feature_names_out` (adding the transformer name prefix). This works with pandas but not polars which does not allow creating a dataframe with duplicate feature names

in addition to the reproducer below, `pytest sklearn/tests/test_pipeline.py::test_feature_union_set_output` fails if we replace "pandas" with "polars" in the test

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import polars as pl
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.pipeline import FeatureUnion
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 4], "b": [1, 2, 3, 4]})
fu = FeatureUnion([("std_1", StandardScaler()), ("std_2", StandardScaler())])
fu.set_output(transform="pandas")
fu.fit_transform(df) # OK

# result:
#
#    std_1__a  std_1__b  std_2__a  std_2__b
# 0 -1.341641 -1.341641 -1.341641 -1.341641
# 1 -0.447214 -0.447214 -0.447214 -0.447214
# 2  0.447214  0.447214  0.447214  0.447214
# 3  1.341641  1.341641  1.341641  1.341641

df = pl.from_pandas(df)
fu.set_output(transform="polars")
fu.fit_transform(df) # ERROR during hstack step as both transformers have output column names ['a', 'b']

# error:
# Traceback (most recent call last):
#     ...
# polars.exceptions.DuplicateError: column with name 'a' has more than one occurrence
```

### Expected Results

dataframe with column names `std_1__a  std_1__b  std_2__a  std_2__b`

### Actual Results

title: BUG: `str.replace("", "")` sometimes hangs up the console
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [ ] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
>>> import pandas as pd
>>> c = pd.Series(["1234,56"])
>>> c.str.replace("", "")
```

### Issue Description

I have found a very weird bug while replacing strings with `str.replace`. The minimal example above _in some cases_ will hang up the console and make it completely unresponsive. It even catches kill signals so I have to terminate the console from elsewhere. 

The problem is that I can't seem to be able to reproduce it consistently: it seems to run properly in some cases, and then it will randomly hang up everything. I have confirmed the same bug with other people, and it's also hanging up the CI in one of my repos (https://github.com/skrub-data/skrub/actions/runs/23753708049/job/69302405774?pr=1772), so it's not only my machine that is acting up. 

I tried both pandas 3.0.0 and pandas 3.0.1, and had the same issue with both versions. 

### Expected Behavior

The correct substitution:

```python
>>> import pandas as pd
>>> c = pd.Series(["1234,56"])
>>> c.str.replace("", "")
0    1234,56
dtype: str

title: The default token pattern in CountVectorizer breaks Indic sentences into non-sensical tokens
body:
### Describe the bug

The default `token_pattern` in `CountVectorizer` is `r"(?u)\b\w\w+\b"` which tokenizes Indic texts in a wrong way - breaks whitespace tokenized words into multiple chunks and even omits several valid characters. The resulting vocabulary doesn't make any sense !

Is this the expected behaviour?

Sample code is pasted in the sections below

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```
import sklearn
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import CountVectorizer

tel = ["ప్రధానమంత్రిని కలుసుకున్నారు"]
hin = ["आधुनिक मानक हिन्दी"]
eng = ["They met the Prime Minister"]

cvect = CountVectorizer(
    ngram_range=(1, 1),
    max_features=None,
    min_df=1,
    strip_accents=None,
)
cvect.fit(tel + hin + eng)
print(cvect.vocabulary_)
```

### Expected Results

```
{'ప్రధానమంత్రిని': 9, 'కలుసుకున్నారు': 8, 'आधुनिक': 5, 'मानक': 6, 'हिन्दी': 7, 'they': 4, 'met': 0, 'the': 3, 'prime': 2, 'minister': 1}
```

### Actual Results

```
{'రధ': 9, 'నమ': 8, 'కల': 7, 'आध': 5, 'नक': 6, 'they': 4, 'met': 0, 'the': 3, 'prime': 2, 'minister': 1}
```


### Versions

```
System:
    python: 3.11.10 (main, Oct  3 2024, 07:29:13) [GCC 11.2.0]
executable: miniconda3/envs/lolm/bin/python

title: [Bug] Model Picker Configuration Not Persisted to `chatLanguageModels.json` — Third-Party Providers Cannot Receive User-Configured Model Settings
body:
## Summary

When a user adjusts model parameters (e.g. **Reasoning Effort**, **Context Size**, **Service Tier**) via the **Chat Model Picker dropdown**, the values are correctly persisted for the current chat editor, but third-party `LanguageModelChatProvider` extensions receive the previously persisted value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default — not the user-selected value — in `modelConfiguration` at request time. The user-visible settings do not flow into the API surface used by non-Copilot providers.

---

## Regression Window

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Last Known Good** | VS Code **1.124.2** (2026-06-10) |
| **First Known Bad** | VS Code **1.125.0** (2026-06-17) — also **1.125.1** |
| **Root Cause PR** | [#321011](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/321011) — *Fix model configurations syncing across editors and locations* |
| **Underlying Issue** | [#320393](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/320393) — *Context length selector changes on all open editors* |
| **Milestone** | [1.125.0](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/milestone/424) |
| **Files Changed** | `+725 / −58` across ~8 files |

### What the PR Did

PR [#321011](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/321011) introduced **per-editor scoped model configuration** to fix a bug where changing context size in one editor affected all other open editors. The key additions were:

- **`chatModelConfigurationStore.ts`** — Per-editor `IStorageService` bucket (resolves per `(location, sessionType)` key)
- **`chatModelConfigurationLogic.ts`** — Resolution logic: `resolveModelConfiguration()`, `computeStoredConfiguration()`, `filterConfigurationToSchema()`, `extractSchemaDefaults()`
- **`modelPickerActionItem.ts`** — `IModelConfigurationAccess` interface (read/write/actions contract)
- **Modifications to `chatInputPart.ts`** — Routes model picker reads/writes through `ChatModelConfigurationStore`
- **Modifications to `chatService.ts` / `chatServiceImpl.ts`** — Passes `userSelectedModelConfiguration` through to requests
- **Modifications to `languageModels.ts`** — Extracted `createModelConfigurationActions()` helper

### What Broke

Before 1.125.0, model picker wrote configuration **directly** via `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` → both the global `_modelConfigurations` Map and `chatLanguageModels.json`. After the change, the primary write targets the per-editor `IStorageService` bucket, and only *mirrors* to the global service as a secondary operation. The mirror write (`ChatModelConfigurationStore.setModelConfiguration()` → `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()`) has a **hidden dependency**: it requires the model's metadata to already exist in `_modelCache`. When `_modelCache` is not yet populated (e.g. provider registered asynchronously), `setModelConfiguration()` silently exits without writing anything.

| | **1.124.2 (Good)** | **1.125.0 (Bad)** |
|---|---|---|
| **Primary write** | `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` | `ChatModelConfigurationStore._applyLocalModelConfiguration()` → per-editor bucket |
| **Global mirror** | N/A (it was the primary) | `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` → ⚠️ depends on `_modelCache` hit |
| **Copilot reads** | `ChatRequest.modelConfiguration` ← ChatInputPart | `ChatRequest.modelConfiguration` ← `ChatModelConfigurationStore` ✅ |
| **3rd-party provider reads** | `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration` ← `_modelConfigurations` ✅ | Same path, but `_modelConfigurations` may be empty ⚠️ |

---

## Environment

- **VS Code Version**: ≥ 1.125.0 (Insiders / Stable)
- **OS**: All platforms
- **Affected APIs**: `vscode.lm.registerLanguageModelChatProvider` / `LanguageModelChatProvider`

---

## Root Cause Analysis

### The Silent Failure Path

| | **Channel ①: Per-Editor Bucket** | **Channel ②: Global `_modelConfigurations`** | **Channel ③: `chatLanguageModels.json`** |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Storage** | `IStorageService` KV store | In-memory `Map<string, IStringDictionary>` | Filesystem JSON |
| **Written by** | `ChatModelConfigurationStore._applyLocalModelConfiguration()` | `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` | `LanguageModelsConfigurationService` |
| **Write reliability** | ✅ Always succeeds | ⚠️ Depends on `_modelCache` hit | ⚠️ Depends on Channel ② |
| **Read by Copilot** | ✅ `userSelectedModelConfiguration` | ❌ Not used | ❌ Not used |
| **Read by 3rd-party LM providers** | ❌ Not accessible | ✅ `getModelConfiguration()` | ❌ Not read directly |

### The Silent Failure

The critical issue is in [`LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/languageModels.ts):

```typescript
async setModelConfiguration(modelId: string, values: IStringDictionary<unknown>): Promise<void> {
    const metadata = this._modelCache.get(modelId);
    if (!metadata) {
        return;  // ⚠️ SILENT RETURN — no write, no error, no warning!
    }
    // ...
    // 1. Update _modelConfigurations (in-memory)
    // 2. Write to chatLanguageModels.json
    // 3. Fire onDidChangeLanguageModels event
}
```

When `_modelCache` does not contain the model metadata (e.g., because the extension provider registered asynchronously and the cache was not yet populated, or was cleared during model re-resolution), the method **silently exits** without:
- Updating `_modelConfigurations` (the cache from which third-party providers read)  
- Writing to `chatLanguageModels.json`  
- Throwing or logging any error  

### Why Copilot Is Unaffected

Copilot participates via the `ChatParticipant` API. Its request path receives configuration through `ChatRequest.modelConfiguration`, which is sourced from `ChatInputPart.getModelConfiguration()` → `ChatModelConfigurationStore.getModelConfiguration()` — the **per-editor bucket (Channel ①)**:

```typescript
// chatServiceImpl.ts
modelConfiguration: options?.userSelectedModelConfiguration
    ?? (options?.userSelectedModelId
        ? this.languageModelsService.getModelConfiguration(options.userSelectedModelId)
        : undefined),
```

The primary value comes from `userSelectedModelConfiguration` (Channel ①), which is always available. The fallback to `getModelConfiguration()` (Channel ②) is only used when that primary source is absent.

### Why Third-Party Providers Are Affected

Third-party `LanguageModelChatProvider` implementations receive configuration through `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration`, which is populated from `LanguageModelsService.getModelConfiguration()` — the **global `_modelConfigurations` cache (Channel ②)**:

```typescript
// languageModelsService.sendChatRequest()
async sendChatRequest(modelId, from, messages, options, token) {
    const configuration = this.getModelConfiguration(modelId);
    const mergedOptions = configuration
        ? { ...options, configuration: { ...configuration, ...options.configuration } }
        : options;
    return provider.sendChatRequest(modelId, messages, from, mergedOptions, token);
}
```

If Channel ② was never populated with the user's latest picker selection (due to the silent failure above), `modelConfiguration` arrives as the previously persisted value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default.

---

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install a third-party extension that registers a `LanguageModelChatProvider` with a `configurationSchema` (e.g. one providing `reasoningEffort` and `contextSize` properties).
2. Open the Chat view and select the third-party model from the model picker.
3. In the model picker dropdown, change **Reasoning Effort** or **Context Size** to a non-default value (e.g., `max`).
4. Send a chat request using this model.
5. Observe in the extension's handler that `options.modelConfiguration` contains the previously persisted value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default — never the value just selected in the picker.

### Expected

- Third-party providers should receive the user-selected configuration values from the model picker in `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration`.

### Actual

- `options.modelConfiguration` at request time contains the value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default, not the value the user just selected in the model picker.
- The model picker UI shows the correct selected value (because it reads from the per-editor bucket), but the actual request uses the persisted/default value visible to third-party providers.

---

## Proposed Fix

### Short-term

At minimum, `setModelConfiguration()` should **log a warning** when `_modelCache` is missing a model, so the silent failure is visible. Additionally, consider retrying the write when the model becomes available:

```typescript
async setModelConfiguration(modelId: string, values: IStringDictionary<unknown>): Promise<void> {
    const metadata = this._modelCache.get(modelId);
    if (!metadata) {
        this._logService.warn(`[LM] Cannot persist configuration for '${modelId}': model not yet resolved in cache`);
        // Optionally: queue the write for when the model resolves
        return;
    }
    // ...
}
```

### Long-term

1. **Unify the two configuration channels** so there is a single source of truth for per-model configuration, accessible equally to Copilot and third-party providers.
2. **Expose per-editor model configuration** to extensions through a stable API, so `LanguageModelChatProvider` implementations can access the user's current selection without relying on the global cache.
3. **Make `chatLanguageModels.json` or a unified service the canonical persistence layer** for all model configuration, ensuring that changes from the model picker are always written to the file/service and are consistently read by all consumers, including third-party providers.

---

## Related Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/chatModelConfigurationStore.ts` | Per-editor scoped storage (Channel ①) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/chatModelConfigurationLogic.ts` | Resolution, filtering, schema extraction |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/languageModels.ts` | `setModelConfiguration()` / `getModelConfiguration()` / `sendChatRequest()` (Channel ②) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/languageModelsConfigurationService.ts` | `chatLanguageModels.json` read/write (Channel ③) |
| `src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostLanguageModels.ts` | Extension host: `$startChatRequest` — bridges `modelConfiguration` to provider |
| `src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadLanguageModels.ts` | Main thread: registers provider & proxies `sendChatRequest` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts` | Chat service: merges `userSelectedModelConfiguration` → `modelConfiguration` |

---

## Impact

This bug affects **all third-party `LanguageModelChatProvider` extensions** that declare a `configurationSchema` and rely on `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration` to receive user-configured model parameters (reasoning effort, context size, service tier, etc.).

title: BUG: `DataFrame.any`/`all(skipna=False)` raises ValueError instead of returning NA
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd

s = pd.Series([False, None], dtype="boolean")
df = s.to_frame("a")

s.any(skipna=False)
df.any(skipna=False)
```

### Issue Description

`DataFrame.any(skipna=False)` and `DataFrame.all(skipna=False)` raise `ValueError: cannot convert float NaN to bool` on numpy-nullable columns (`boolean`, `Int*`, `UInt*`, `Float*`) whenever the reduction would propagate `pd.NA`.

The corresponding `Series.any/all(skipna=False)` reductions on the same data correctly return `pd.NA`.

`DataFrame.any(skipna=False)` and `DataFrame.all(skipna=False)` should return a `Series` with nullable boolean dtype (`boolean`) containing `pd.NA` when the corresponding `Series.any/all(skipna=False)` reduction on the same column returns `pd.NA`.

title: BUG: Trees: minor bug in `NaN` detection leading to incorrect split.
body:
### Describe the bug

The `break` [here](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/afc095a7b17d5528d62f55f07cab39f963299c78/sklearn/tree/_utils.pyx#L85) is wrong, and in some edge cases it will prevent `_any_isnan_axis0` detecting NaNs for some features. Then because of that, the splitter won't considers splits with NaNs on the left (because it's not aware this feature has NaNs).

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```Python
import numpy as np
from numpy import nan
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeRegressor

X = np.array([
    [nan, nan],
    [1, 0],
    [1, 1]
])

y = np.array([0, 0, 1])

tree = DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=1).fit(X, y)
assert (tree.tree_.impurity[1:] == 0).all()
```

### Expected Results

The best split is found => No assertion error

The best split: `X[:, 1] < 0.5` and missing values to the left node.

### Actual Results

Assertion error.

title: average_precision_score not working as expected
body:
### Describe the bug

When compute AP with average_precision_score, I get unexpected results. The y_scores (output from the models) are very low for positive samples, so my AP should be very low. Instead I get a perfect 1.0 AP score.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
from sklearn.metrics import average_precision_score

# labels
y_true = [1, 1, 1, 1, 1]  # 5 positives
# Predicted scores
y_scores = [0.1, 0.3, 0.1, 0.2, 0.1]  # Model's confidence in predictions

# Calculate average precision
average_precision = average_precision_score(y_true, y_scores)

title: `parametrize_with_checks` fails if custom estimator implements `__call__`
body:
### Describe the bug

Title.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
from sklearn.utils.estimator_checks import parametrize_with_checks


class MyEstimator:
    """Dummy estimator."""

    def get_params(self, *, deep=True):
        return {}

    def set_params(self, **kwargs):
        pass

    def fit(self, X, y=None):
        return self

    def fit_transform(self, X, y=None):
        return self.fit(X, y).transform(X)

    def transform(self, X):
        return X

    def __call__(self, X):
        return self.transform(X)


@parametrize_with_checks([MyEstimator()])
def test_sklearn_compatibility(estimator, check):
    check(estimator)
```

### Expected Results

The tests should run.

### Actual Results

Parametrizing the tests fails, because `_get_check_estimator_ids` thinks it's a function tries to look up `obj.__name__`, which doesn't exist.

title: BUG: Inconsistent nan to None behaviour in replace() with scalar vs list value
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

branches = pd.DataFrame(
    data={
        "name": ["river_1", "river_2", "river_3", "river_4", "river_4-1", "pipe_51_E11", "pipe_51_E12", "pipe_52_E0"],
        "branchtype": [0,0,0,0,0,2,2,2],
        "sourceCompartmentName": [np.nan, np.nan, np.nan, np.nan, np.nan, "manhole_987_generated", "manhole_988_generated", "manhole_989_generated"],
        "targetCompartmentName": [np.nan, np.nan, np.nan, np.nan, np.nan, "manhole_988_generated",
                                  "manhole_967_generated", "manhole_990_generated"],
    },
index=[0,1,2,3,4,1002,1003,1004],
)

# print the dtype, str in pandas 2 (?) and <StringDtype(na_value=nan)> in pandas 3
# using repr since StringDtype is also printed as str otherwise
print(branches["sourceCompartmentName"].dtype.__repr__())

# does not work in pandas 3, since None is interpreted as the dype na_value
branches = branches.replace(np.nan, None)
# a useful workaround that does work
# branches = branches.replace([np.nan], [None])

print(branches.to_dict("records"))
```

### Issue Description

Context: Originally reported in https://github.com/Deltares/HYDROLIB-core/issues/1107. In our code, we would regularly initiating an object of some class (hydrolib-core BranchModel) with some parameters set to `None`. These are then considered empty/notset, consistent with many other class/method argument defaults.

This is why we need to have `None` values in our dataframes with string dtype columns (and not np.nan values). Pandas 2 supports both these values, pandas 3 implemented stringdtype to be more robust. This has a na_value argument/property, which is set to np.nan per default. This na_value cannot be set to `None` since it is considered an empty argument (which sort of makes sense to me).

However, we noticed that in pandas 3 it is actually possible to convert `np.nan` to `None`  by adding square brackets:
- `branches.replace(np.nan, None)` does not replace the np.nan values
- `branches.replace([np.nan], [None])` replaces the np.nan values with None

This is quite nice for our usecase, since we can use this as a workaround. However, this seems inconsistent and might be considered as a bug. If this is intended behaviour, that is also fine since we can then trust that the behaviour will not change in the future.

### Expected Behavior

One of these two:
- consistent behaviour between the two methods
- confirmation that this is intended and will not change in the forseeable future. Or any other suggestion to avoid the need for a workaround.
